text.title: FIRST TIME in AMSTERDAM, NETHERLANDS...it was more chaotic than we expected
text.u: Good morning from Amsterdam, you guys. I
am super stoked to be here. This city
has been on my bucket list for such a
long time. Today we are me. Look who's
behind you. What?
Oh, mom and dad. Hey. Hey. Hello.
Welcome.
Come on in here. Oh, that was so cute.
Hi, guys. Come on over here. All right.
So, we are very excited for the next
several days. We're traveling with my
parents, Tom and Jackie. Some of you
guys probably know him as well. And no
better way to start than here in the
unique city of Amsterdam.
Oh,
[Music]
thank you so much. I appreciate that. No
worries. Have a good holiday.
So, we arrived here yesterday from Paris
on the Euro Star. We used our Euro
passes, so that was quick and easy. I
love taking the fast trains. And Tom and
Jackie arrived here from Spain. They've
also been exploring a bit of Europe,
doing their own thing, and now we're all
together here in Amsterdam. We're
actually staying a little bit outside of
Amsterdam in the cutest lodging and more
affordable. So, we can show you guys
that a little bit later on in the video.
One of my favorite things about being
here in Europe is that yesterday we were
just surrounded by people speaking
French, French architecture, baguettes,
macarons, you know, all the French
things. And then we've traveled just
like a few hours over and everything is
so Dutch, all the architecture, the
windmills, the canals, the tulips, and
yeah, we're just really excited to be
here together as a family and getting
our first impressions of the city.
Heat. Heat.
[Music]
[Music]
[Music]
Oh, what did you got? Got us a treat.
These are called chest mini Dutch
pancakes. And we got the original with
butter and sugar. And I got two forks
just for me and you, Jackie. All right.
No sharing. No sharing.
Crispy on the outside. Buttery and
sugar. Has really good flavor. Yeah, I
like it. And we came to a coffee shop,
so we're going to have our pancakes and
coffee. Cheers. It was funny when I was
asking the guys at the pancake shop. I
was like, "Oh, is there a restaurant
nearby?" And he's recommended this cafe.
It's called Momento. And he's like, "But
make sure it's a coffee store or a cafe,
not a coffee shop." Cuz that is very
different.
[Music]
What's your name? Yura. Yura. Yeah.
That's a cool name, man. That's unique.
Where you from? Here. Are you? Yeah.
Yeah. You must love it. Such a beautiful
city. It's a nice city. What were you
telling me about the wall? But it's it's
the original 1680s 1680s one. So that's
interesting. Like you can see here the
the wood.
Oh yeah.
That's awesome. And thanks for nice to
meet you.
So as we're walking by all these canals,
you you'll notice the houses are super
narrow, like super skinny and tall.
That's because back in the 17th century,
the houses were taxed based on the width
of the houses. So the less wide, the
more tax breaks they got, the more money
they saved.
[Music]
What do you think so far, man? Is it
living up to the hype? This is better
than anything I expected. True. I expect
the beautiful canal and the you know the
old buildings but the atmosphere is
overwhelming for me. It's beautiful.
It's casual. Just feels good. It is very
relaxed.
[Music]
[Music]
This is an Frank's house. Obviously, as
you can tell by the line, it is one of
the biggest tourist draws in Amsterdam.
Have to probably buy your tickets a
couple weeks in advance. Uh we weren't
able to get in for a tour, but kind of
glad we didn't because it looks like
there's a lot of renovations going on.
It's hard to get a feel of what the
house really looks like from the
outside. Yeah.
[Music]
He's
[Music]
a beautiful.
I went off script. I saw the cutest
little shop. So, I had to pop in and I
bought the cutest ring. Another ring.
And I didn't even tell you guys. I
picked up these other two little rings
in Paris and some earrings. That's my
favorite little souvenir because I can
just put it on and travel with it. It
doesn't take up any space in my luggage.
So,
[Music]
[Music]
So, this city is obviously known for its
canal system, which stretches over 100
km long, and they have over 1,300
bridges, which is three times the amount
that Venice has. It's hard not to
compare the city with Venice. Me and dad
were already talking about it. Just so
much more relaxed, so much more room to
get around.
I want to cry but love you till I die.
The guy we met at the coffee shop
earlier was so sweet. He directed us to
this cute little place which is a
traditional Dutch diner since 1945. And
he told us what to order. Some little
roast beef sandwiches. Tommy got a
salted beef sandwich. And I think
classically you're supposed to get it
with milk. Tommy came back with a glass
of chocolate milk. He went rogue. I
don't think it's the same, but it looks
yummy. It's on the menu. Has to be good.
Mom said it was on the menu. So,
chocolate milk. And we also got a order
of bitter ball.
That's a great recommendation. Love the
vibe of this place. It's super busy.
Looks like a ton of locals. People are
just sitting up at the bar and the
workers are uh humorous. Humorous. It's
good work for it. Cheers, you guys. Look
how delicious that looks. They they
serve it to you like right away. Sam's
making fun of me. How good does that
look? I haven't had chocolate milk since
like the second grade.
So simple, but oh so good. Good. That's
good beef. And not only that, the
mustard's amazing for some reason, isn't
it?
Obviously, I think the the salted one is
better. Look, it's his almost gone
already.
The bitter ball are like a Dutch snack
food. They're like ve or ragu meatballs
I think in the sauce and then deep
fried. So I think it's a little bit
misleading this crispy exterior. I think
they're going to be not so crispy on the
inside. And you dip them in mustard
[Music]
crispy.
That is so good. It's like a like a
stew. A deep fried stew ball.
Interesting. Why does Sammy get to go
first all the time? You can tell I came
from a family with a lot of siblings. I
kind of feel like I'm in Willy Wonka
land. You know, when you bite into it,
it's a full meal. Roast beef, potatoes,
and gravy. It's so true.
Hey, the second course is coming up.
Roast beef and a baked potato.
That cafe was felt so authentic. The
ladies were so sweet working there, too.
And the food was so good. So good. We
had to go back for seconds.
[Music]
What do you think of the alternate
thinker? Well, I think that I just saw
the original in Paris 2 days ago.
[Music]
So, after all that salted beef, we're a
little thirsty. So, it's time for some
beers. We wouldn't be hanging out
together. It wouldn't feel right if we
didn't have some beers now, right? Back
with the crew. And I was going to take
him to a sweet treat and Jackie was
like, "I think I'm filling a drink on a
terrace." I was like, "That sounds
pretty good, too." Thank you.
[Music]
Cheers, you guys. Amsterdam. Amsterdam.
One of the things I was most excited for
about coming to Amsterdam is or Holland
in general is their shroop waffles. This
is like I love having these back at
home, but this is fresh, so I'm super
stoked about that. Look like mom got a
fancy one. Chocolate and raspberries.
Amazing. Let's dig in. I feel like
mine's not as good as the ones I bought
from the store. The raspberry is
amazing.
I know that's not what I'm supposed to
be commenting on, but yeah.
[Music]
We've just come to walk through Vondo
Park. It's so nice during sunset hour
right now. It is busy. You got to very
much stay vigilant with the bicycles and
the runners, etc. But it's so nice
because I love like the river with the
tulip beds and the parakeetses in the
trees. Yeah, great way to spend the
evening. I don't ever think I've seen a
river that has like floating flower beds
in it. And this river just has like
floating tullet beds. It's like the most
unique, beautiful, colorful thing. It's
so adorable, right? Very unique. It's
very cool. Yeah.
[Music]
[Music]
Nice size boat.
So, we're ending our day. We rented our
own boat. It was only €80 a couple,
which is really nice. We looked in a lot
of the group tours, but they look busy.
It's just nice having our own boat to
explore all the canals we want. We
bought some snack seed as well. Now
we're just relaxing. We're going to wait
until the lights come on, too, to see
all the reflective lights and Amsterdam
at night. So, I think we timed it
perfectly. How you feeling, Capitan?
Feeling good. Okay,
we went to the grocery store and picked
up some drinks.
Little gin and tonic and a can. It's our
favorite. And tang, too. Cheers. Tanker
should sponsor us. That's terrible. I
didn't say that. No, they should. That'd
be amazing. So, we also picked up some
snacks. Some nuts, salami, olives, and
cheese. Perfect. And some chips and
things like that. So, yeah. Cheers.
[Music]
So Sammy wasn't very happy with me that
I booked a boat cruise so late and she
didn't want to get her own boat. But
aren't you glad we did now? Yes. Only
because of the price. I was like €80 a
couple only. It was 60 a piece to go on
a group tour with a bunch of other
people. But we saw the ones that you
could pay 30 or 40 and it's it's worth
the extra money. Oh man, so nice. The
timing too is spectacular. The lighting
is so nice. I love snacks on a boat. I
just it's just so just love it. Snacks
on a boat. Can't beat it.
[Music]
This is the life. Yeah. Great move.
Perfect. Hey, is there a better way to
see Amsterdam than this? I don't think
so. No.
Second Stroop Waffle today. The store
brand. I like these better.
[Music]
All right. Lights are coming on.
[Music]
[Music]
It's so pretty. Oh, it's so nice.
I am
[Music]
without the night.
[Music]
We're back. It was great. So fun, right?
Everybody enjoyed it. Fabulous.
Beautiful and really nice. Okay, now the
big question. Do we want to walk through
the red light district?
Red light district. I mean, you seem
hankering for it. I just want to see it.
She can't go to Amsterdam without going
to the red light district. And nobody
else I'd rather go with than my parents
and my wife.
[Music]
everything.
Okay, we just walked through the
district. Many pretty ladies, right?
Yeah, just a bunch of pretty ladies and
windows and lots of lights. Lots of
street food, actually, too. Such a great
day here in Amsterdam. We'll see you
guys tomorrow. We're going to explore,
show you guys where we're staying at,
and go to Cooking Hall. Yeah. See some
of the flowers. Good night, guys.
[Music]
We can be
anything.
We can be
anything.
[Music]
Good morning from our beautiful place in
Hiligum. Hillome. Hillome. So, we're
staying about 45 minutes outside of
Amsterdam. It takes us two trains to get
in, which is what we did yesterday. We
explored all over Amsterdam. And then
today, we are going to Kenhoff. We're
going to go see all the tulips. But this
is our beautiful place. We're right on a
canal. We have these beautiful tulip
fields that are covered by plastic cuz I
think they're trying to save the bulbs
for the parade next week is what our
host told us. But some of the fields
over there are just absolutely vibrant.
And we have canoes here that we can put
in the canal and go like canoeing around
if we wanted to get up close with the
tulips. And look, there's ducks and
birds. And there's swans here. It's so
peaceful and relaxing. And the Airbnb is
massive for four people. So that's
really comfortable for us. And the price
was so good. I think it was $500 a
couple for four nights. So for
Amsterdam, that's a pretty good deal. I
think we're outside Amsterdam. We're
outside of Amsterdam. It's a pretty good
deal. And the place also comes with
bikes. And today we're going to be
biking into Kukenhoff. Yay. So, they
only had three bikes at our Airbnb, so I
rented an electric bike here. It was €20
for the day. And the guy who was working
there recommended another bike path you
can go on. There's like a tulip barn you
can go check out, a museum as well. So,
we'll see how much we explore today
around our spot here in Hong Kong.
Yeah. La.
So nice being in a place where it's so
bike friendly.
I feel right around. Wow, look how
pretty. So lucky to be here this time of
year, right? Perfect timing.
No, it really worked out perfectly. The
flowers are still in bloom and the last
two days have been warm, so they're
extra blooming.
Ooh la la. I like how beautiful.
I like the things you do. I like it when
I'm with you.
I did it. You're so dramatic. Good job,
though. I was so scared. The biggest
white team.
TMI.
I don't like biking.
[Music]
I can't believe the cars coming in here
and all the bikes. It is so packed with
people. People love their tulips
apparently.
I'm super glad we actually bike though
cuz the traffic so backed up a long
ways.
I like it when you
do. I like it when you got my first
picture.
Snap it.
I like it when I'm with you.
La.
[Music]
So, really nice of ladies. Not really
our thing. Very pretty. Yeah, but I did
see someone carrying a beer around as
they're looking at flowers. Okay,
there's hope yet. So, there might be
might be our next destination.
[Music]
[Music]
So, I am blown away by the crowd. So, I
had to show you guys and get it up high.
[Music]
I feel tricked. I know Tommy does. He He
looked at me earlier. He was like, I
feel like you tricked me cuz I told him
we're coming to a place with beautiful
flowers, but he didn't know like how
like viral Disneyland. He didn't know
how viral this place was. So, it is
very, very crowded here. I don't get it.
It's beautiful. The flowers are so
pretty. Everything's so The weather's
perfect around the river. The like it's
just a great atmosphere. But there are
shoulderto-shoulder people here. So,
bars are looking better.
Heat.
[Music]
Heat.
Did
[Music]
you have a good time? I did. I enjoyed
all the beautiful flowers. My favorites
are the ones that are mixed and you can
see all the different ones together. Me,
too. Rather than just the rows of one
solid color. Yeah, it's a bit crowded
for us. Short and sweet visit. We're
going to hop back on our bikes.
Any takeaways, Mom? Oh, everything was
stunning. And it's just imagine you can
come back in two more weeks and get a
different type of viewing, which is
crazy. Yeah, that's awesome. Okay, we're
here for a little over an hour. Too
long. Way too crowded. All right, time
to get out of here. Over there on the
right. Yep. This way.
[Music]
You see all the cars lined up.
So, the guy I rented my bike from
recommended this castle, which we're
walking up to now. I think it's €10 to
get inside, but we just want to look at
the grounds and see the outside of it.
Looks very cool.
[Music]
Very
pretty, but chaotic, right? So chaotic.
So many cars, not big enough roads.
Definitely not for a weak biker as well.
You guys want to bail on it? Pretty
busy, right? I want to bail. Okay.
We've come to Zor, a nice local cafe
here. The whole place is rented out for
a birthday party. So, we have the little
back back table in the corner. We just
met the owner. His name's Nigel. Very
sweet man. And he recommended a IPA.
Actually, it's called Insomnia. Has like
a textured can. Look how cool that
looks. That's nice. He was very excited
to share it with us. So, me and dad are
excited to try it. Cheers. Cheers.
Cheers. Cheers. Cheers.
Yeah, that's fantastic. Isn't that good?
So fruity, but cheesy. Definitely a
hazy. Yeah, hazy, which is our favorite
citrus. How did he know? Super citrusy.
Okay, thank you, Nigel.
I got the meat platter. Looks like we
have some steak, some pork ribs, and
some chicken saté. Oh, yeah. With fries.
With fries and a salad, which are both
refillable. Sammy got a big spicy burger
with some fries. I love that the salad
is refillable.
Okay, ended our day with some ice cream.
I got the peanut and dark chocolate 70%.
Dark chocolate and cherry. Cherry was
her favorite. Cherry's the best.
Peanut's really good, too. And the dark
chocolate, they're all amazing. Such a
great first time here in Amsterdam and
our little town here. Um, tomorrow we're
actually going to do a food tour as
well. So, we'll see you guys then. Thank
you for watching. Cheers. Cheers. Heat.
[Music]
[Applause]
[Music]
Heat.
[Music]
